body.title: Device channel posts appear in web UI per-device tab
body.result: channel.py _ws_push added; Granny posts route to comms://granny-weatherwax with WS broadcast
body.description:
Granny's _post_channel() writes to Postgres channel_messages only. The web UI only shows messages that go through the web server's WebSocket hub. So Granny's dispatch/pickup events are invisible in the web UI — her tab exists (from agent registration) but is always empty.

Proposed shape: add POST /api/channel/push endpoint to web server: {"session_id": "comms://granny-weatherwax", "author": "granny-weatherwax", "content": "..."}. This calls agent_send() internally — broadcasts to WS clients in that session. Update _post_channel in Granny and channel.py post_to_channel to also POST to this endpoint when UC_BASE is reachable.

**Affected files:** devices/web_server/server.py, devices/granny/daemon.py, unseen_university/channel.py
**Design rules:** graceful degradation — if web server unreachable, Postgres write still happens; WS push is best-effort
**Scope boundary:** Granny events visible in web UI; not a general Postgres→WS bridge
**Completion criteria:** Granny dispatches a ticket → event appears in comms://granny-weatherwax tab in web UI within 5 seconds
